title: Quality Control
text: Date: 2026-Apr-16 06:39:39 document provides Quality Control (QC) report R package confirm fulfills criteria required “released” status. QC report contains following information: Specifications (specs): can attached every test user adds. Traceability matrix: Contains test cases passed, failed, skipped expectations. Uncovered undeclared specs Session Info System Configuration

title: Quality Control
text: traceability matrix tests point specification included. Test cases can contain several expectations test considered: passed expectations test pass. failed least one expectation test fails. skipped least one expectation test skipped. test can failed skipped.

title: dv.edish
text: eDISH module supports assessment drug-induced liver injury means (modified) evaluation Drug-Induced Serious Hepatotoxicity plot.

title: dv.edish
text: eDISH module shows scatter plot depicting correlation peak values aminotransferase parameter liver function parameter subject level. user can control following user interface drop-plot options: Treatment arm selection. aminotransferase parameter displayed x-axis (e.g. Alanine Aminotransferase Aspartate Aminotransferase). liver function parameter displayed y-axis (e.g. Total Bilirubin Prothrombin Intl. Normalized Ratio) window liver function parameter value considered relation aminotransferase value. Whether parameters displayed multiples either upper limits normal (resulting eDISH plot) corresponding subject’s baseline values (resulting mDISH plot). Horizontal vertical reference lines indicating Hy’s law multiples. Whether include subjects based baseline value within aminotransferase threshold. Whether plot subject aminotransferase values visit, lines connecting points visits. Lower /upper values x- y-axis ranges. specified values determined data. hovering point following pop-information displayed: Subject identifier. Arm. Normalized aminotransferase peak value, visit, date. Associated normalized Alkaline Phosphatase value categorized. R-ratio categorized. Normalized liver function parameter peak value, visit, date. Time days aminotransferase liver function dates. Negative days indicates liver function date aminotransferase date. Normalized Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP/ULN ALP/Baseline) categories shown ≤ 2 > 2. R-ratio (specific ULN) calculated R = Normalized aminotransferase / Normalized ALP: R ≥ 5 Hepatocellular - aminotransferase dominates; typical Hy’s Law R ≤ 2 Cholestatic - ALP dominates 2 < R < 5 Mixed - aminotransferase ALP elevated

title: dv.edish
text: following example shows set simple DaVinci app means {dv.manager}. app contains eDISH module display dummy data provided {pharmaverseadam} package: Note module expects two datasets: Demographics dataset (e.g. dm adsl) including, least, variables containing following information: Unique subject identifier (e.g. USUBJID) Arm (e.g. ACTARM) Laboratory Test Results dataset (e.g. lb adlb) including, least, variables containing following information: Unique subject identifier (e.g. USUBJID) Visit (e.g. VISIT) Lab test result name (e.g. LBTEST) Lab test date (e.g. ADT) Numeric lab test result (e.g. LBSTRESN) Lab test reference range upper limit (e.g. LBSTNRHI)
code: dm <- pharmaverseadam::adsl lb <- pharmaverseadam::adlb    module_list <- list(   "edish" = dv.edish::mod_edish(     module_id = "edish",     subject_level_dataset_name = "dm",     lab_dataset_name = "lb",     lb_date_var = "ADT",     arm_default_vals = c("Xanomeline Low Dose", "Xanomeline High Dose"),     baseline_visit_val = "SCREENING 1"   ) )  dv.manager::run_app(   data = list("demo" = list("dm" = dm, "lb" = lb)),   module_list = module_list,   filter_data = "dm" )

what: Derive required variables for plotting — derive_req_vars
title: Derive required variables for plotting — derive_req_vars
text: Derive required variables plotting

title: Derive required variables for plotting — derive_req_vars
code: derive_req_vars(   dataset,   subjectid_var,   arm_var,   visit_var,   baseline_visit_val,   lb_test_var,   at_choices,   tbili_choice,   norm_ref_type,   alp_choice,   lb_date_var,   lb_result_var,   ref_range_upper_lim_var,   by_visit,   window_days )

title: Derive required variables for plotting — derive_req_vars
text: dataset [data.frame] data frame containing data prepare_initial_data(). subjectid_var [character(1)] Name variable containing unique subject IDs. arm_var [character(1)] Name variable containing arm/treatment information. visit_var [character(1)] Name variable containing visit information. baseline_visit_val [character(1)] String indicating visit used baseline visit. lb_test_var [character(1)] Name variable containing laboratory test parameter. at_choices [character(1+)] Character vector specifying possible choices aminotransferase laboratory test parameter x-axis. tbili_choice [character(1)] Character vector specifying choice total bilirubin laboratory test parameter y-axis. norm_ref_type [character(1)] String indicating normalization reference type, either "ULN" "Baseline". alp_choice [character(1) | NULL] Character vector specifying choice alkaline phosphatase laboratory test parameter. lb_date_var [character(1)] Name variable (Date POSIXt class) containing laboratory test date. lb_result_var [character(1)] Name variable containing results laboratory test. ref_range_upper_lim_var [character(1)] Name variable containing reference range upper limits. by_visit [logical(1)] flag indicate whether plot aminotransferase values visit. window_days [integer(1) | NULL] Window number days considered peaks.

title: Derive required variables for plotting — derive_req_vars
text: data frame following derived variables: .ref_val: ULN baseline reference value normalization. .visit_at: Visit peak aminotransferase value. .date_at: Date peak aminotransferase value. .norm_at: Normalized peak aminotransferase value. .visit_tbili: Visit peak total bilirubin value. .date_tbili: Date peak total bilirubin value. .norm_tbili: Normalized peak total bilirubin value. .norm_alp: Normalized alkaline phosphotase value visit aminotransferase value. .offset_days: Number days aminotransferase visit total bilirubin visit. .norm_ref_type: Normalization reference type, copied norm_ref_type argument.

NULL, action taken.

eDISH module — mod_edish
text: mod_edish() displays (modified) evaluation Drug-Induced Serious Hepatotoxicity (eDISH/mDISH) plot support assessment drug-induced liver injury (DILI). scatter plot depicts correlation peak values aminotransferase parameter liver function parameter subject level.

title: eDISH module — mod_edish
code: mod_edish(   module_id,   subject_level_dataset_name,   lab_dataset_name,   lb_date_var,   subjectid_var = "USUBJID",   arm_var = "ACTARM",   arm_default_vals = NULL,   visit_var = "VISIT",   baseline_visit_val = "VISIT 01",   lb_test_var = "LBTEST",   at_choices = c("Alanine Aminotransferase", "Aspartate Aminotransferase"),   at_default_val = "Aspartate Aminotransferase",   tbili_choices = "Bilirubin",   tbili_default_val = "Bilirubin",   alp_choice = "Alkaline Phosphatase",   lb_result_var = "LBSTRESN",   ref_range_upper_lim_var = "LBSTNRHI",   default_by_visit = FALSE,   window_days = NULL,   receiver_id = NULL )

title: eDISH module — mod_edish
text: module_id [character(1)] unique module ID. subject_level_dataset_name [character(1)] Name subject-level dataset used. lab_dataset_name [character(1)] Name laboratory results dataset used. lb_date_var [character(1)] Name variable (Date POSIXt class) containing laboratory test date. subjectid_var [character(1)] Name variable containing unique subject IDs. arm_var [character(1)] Name variable containing arm/treatment information. arm_default_vals [character(1+) | NULL] Vector specifying default value(s) arm selector. visit_var [character(1)] Name variable containing visit information. baseline_visit_val [character(1)] Character indicating visit used baseline visit. lb_test_var [character(1)] Name variable containing laboratory test information. at_choices [character(1+)] Character vector specifying possible choices x-axis aminotransferase laboratory test. at_default_val [character(1) | NULL] Character specifying default x-axis aminotransferase laboratory test choice. tbili_choices [character(1+)] Character vector specifying possible choices y-axis liver function laboratory test (e.g. "Bilirubin", "Prothrombin Intl. Normalized Ratio", etc.). tbili_default_val [character(1) | NULL] Character specifying default y-axis liver function laboratory test choice. alp_choice [character(1) | NULL] Character vector specifying alkaline phosphatase laboratory test choice. lb_result_var [character(1)] Name variable containing results laboratory test. ref_range_upper_lim_var [character(1)] Name variable containing reference range upper limits. default_by_visit [logical(1)] flag indicate default whether plot aminotransferase values visit. window_days [integer(1) | NULL] Window number days considered peaks. receiver_id [character(1) | NULL] Character string defining ID module send subject ID. module must exist module list. default NULL disables communication.

title: eDISH module — mod_edish
text: list containing following elements used dv.manager: ui: UI function dv.edish module. server: server function dv.edish module. module_id: unique identifier.

title: Build a collection of named constants — pack_of_constants
text: Shiny uses strings IDs link UI server elements. E.g: foo_UI(id = ns("foo")) ... foo_server(id = "foo") pattern makes easy programmers fall trap modifying one instance string literal "foo" without modifying rest unaware problem bug hit. also easy mistakes uses "foo" identifier uses (text labels, ...) , often case, parameter explicitly named. One easy fix consists using global variables instead plain string literals. case previous example, mean: ID_FOO <- "foo" foo_UI(ns(ID_FOO)) ... foo_server(ID_FOO) simple addition makes purpose ID_FOO clear also fails gracefully ID_FOO instances updated synchronously along codebase. drawback polluting global namespace identifier variables. easily solved creating container constants, purpose pack_of_constants alias. ID <- pack_of_constants(FOO = "foo", BAR = "bar") ID$FOO "foo" ID$BA NULL

title: dv.edish 2.0.2
text: [USER-FACING] Update early error feedback snippets, communication papo test snippet test snapshot.

title: dv.edish 2.0.1
text: Reinstate UI selection liver function parameter y-axis. Convert mod_edish() lb_date_var mandatory argument removing NULL default.

title: dv.edish 2.0.0
text: Refactor code use {ggiraph} instead {plotly}. Move options sidebar layout drop menu. subject, plot normalized peak TBILI normalized peak ALT/AST values, option plot aminotransferase values visit. Implement spider/path lines aminotransferase values plotted visit. Apply log value axes. Mixing ULN ALT/AST baseline TBILI (vice versa) standard generally make scientific sense DILI analysis, previous eDISH plot allowed control ULN/baseline axis. Update use one selector ULN/baseline applies axes. Show time peaks. Show sequence (peak ALT/AST peak TBILI concerning). Show value ALP (Alkaline Phosphatase) time peak ALT/AST value, flagged ≤ 2 x ULN > 2 x ULN (baseline).

title: dv.edish 1.3.0
text: Add jumping feature. Arm default values can arbitrarily specified long character strings. supports including multiple studies different arm values.

title: dv.edish 1.2.0
text: user can now specify x- y-axis range limits, go default plotly ranges.

title: dv.edish 1.1.0
text: Remove support data dispatchers. Split mod_edish() dataset_names parameter subject-level laboratory datasets. Provide early feedback mod_edish() misconfiguration.

title: dv.edish 1.0.4
text: module ignores now NA values calculating maximum value.

title: dv.edish 1.0.3
text: Initial release dv.edish package GitHub.

title: dv.edish 1.0.2
text: module now able deal multiple values per subject, visit, lab test using maximum value. data display, e.g., due filter settings, module provide meaningful output. module updated show commonly used default values reference lines. x-axis, default value 3, y-axis, default value 2.

title: dv.edish 1.0.1
text: Bugfix: Bookmarking app’s state failed arm parameter selection fixed now.

title: dv.edish 1.0.0
text: Initial version dv.edish.
